original_image_tags: This is the back of a pinkish/salmon-colored file folder or divider page. It contains two punched holes at the top edge. The main body is blank except for multiple upside-down blue ink "RECEIVED" stamps at the bottom edge, indicating processing through FBI Internal Security and FBI U.S. Department of Justice in August 1947.

elements.description: Pinkish-colored paper, likely the back of a document, with two hole-punch marks at the top. Faint, mostly illegible text is visible from the reverse side. Two faint stamped text blocks are visible on the right side, upside down relative to the page orientation, reading 'INTERNAL SECURITY / F. B. I. / RECEIVED / AUG 21 11 01 AM '47' and 'RECEIVED-TAHM / FBI / U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE / RECEIVED / Aug 21 8 52 AM '47'. A third, partially visible stamped text block at the bottom right reads 'INTERNAL SECURITY / F. B. I. / DEPT. OF JUSTICE'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual content: pinkish paper, two hole-punches, upside-down 'RECEIVED' stamps, and the same general stamp text, though B provides more detailed and specific transcriptions of the stamps while A is less detailed.
